general: The relationship between those born in the Year of the Dog and the Year of the Horse is very harmonious. Furthermore, the Year of the Tiger forms a "Yin-Wu-Xu" triad, theoretically leading to a smoother year and the potential for new collaborations. However, in a year of accord with Tai Sui (the Grand Duke Jupiter), fortune often swings between extremes, especially when a crucial element in one's destiny is removed, leading to setbacks. Therefore, about 80% of those born in the Year of the Dog can expect a steady rise in fortune, while about 20% will face fluctuations, requiring caution and vigilance. Besides the harmony with Tai Sui, the Year of the Horse also brings the blessings of auspicious stars such as "San Tai," "Hua Gai," and "Di Jie," all of which will support their careers. "San Tai" symbolizes a staircase, signifying continuous advancement; its brilliance primarily illuminates career advancement, offering employees the potential for promotion through outstanding performance. "Hua Gai" represents the imperial parasol used during imperial processions, signifying high status and respect; in the new year, those born in the Year of the Dog will showcase their talents, particularly in the arts and creative fields, where they will have a greater advantage. However, "Hua Gai" is also associated with religion, carrying connotations of aloofness and solitude, which may not be beneficial for relationships and interpersonal connections. "Di Jie" relates to changes in real estate; your place of residence or work may change during the Year of the Horse, such as relocation, sale, or relocation of your home, office, or shop, or moving abroad or stationing overseas. Although the auspicious stars in the year are quite powerful, the influence of the inauspicious star "Wu Gui" indicates a tendency towards doubt and anxiety. Those born in the Year of the Dog may have doubts about their partners, so more communication and honesty are needed. Due to the influence of inauspicious stars, personal energy will be lower during the Year of the Horse; it is not advisable to travel to remote and dark places to avoid affecting your fortune. "Guan Fu," as its name suggests, refers to lawsuits and litigation. Therefore, in the new year, you must carefully understand the terms and details before signing documents or contracts, and seek professional advice if you have any questions. Business people should also avoid allowing clients to buy on credit and be wary of clients suffering financial losses and lawsuits due to cash flow problems. The appearance of the "Disheveled Hair" star indicates a need to pay attention to the health of elders. This star also signifies disheveled hair, suggesting that one should be more mindful of their appearance during the Year of the Horse. Those intending to change their image must be cautious to avoid criticism and rejection from others. Overall, in this year of harmony with Tai Sui, those born in the Year of the Dog can expect new development opportunities. With the support of auspicious stars representing gradual progress, both career and wealth are expected to improve. However, due to the remaining variables of the harmony with Tai Sui, and the considerable power of inauspicious stars, despite the overall positive trend, those born in the Year of the Dog are advised to proceed cautiously and plan carefully in all matters to ensure safety and avoid potential pitfalls.
career: In the Year of the Horse, those born in the Year of the Dog will be blessed by auspicious stars and will excel in their work, with their careers gradually improving. The "Three Platforms" star symbolizes a rising ladder, indicating outstanding work performance and the potential for promotion through personal connections. Furthermore, the "Hua Gai" star, associated with artistic talent, will grace them, suggesting significant progress and career advancement for those in creative fields such as writing, art, and music. Managers will also showcase their intelligence, but it's important to note that "Hua Gai" may give others the impression of being unapproachable or arrogant; therefore, caution and humility are crucial. On the other hand, those in frontline sales or intermediary roles will face more challenges and need to strengthen communication to enhance cooperation. Additionally, the influence of "Di Jie" suggests potential changes in job positions or roles, including relocation of company bases, frequent business trips, or temporary overseas assignments. It is advised that those born in the Year of the Dog proactively adapt and consider leaving their original place of residence, as this could have a positive impact on their careers. Although the influence of auspicious stars may bring opportunities for promotion and higher positions with greater responsibilities, salary increases will be relatively modest, resulting in minimal actual benefits. Furthermore, the influence of the "Hua Gai" star may lead to weaker interpersonal relationships, making it easier to feel isolated in the work environment and increasing mental stress. It is recommended to adjust your mindset and maintain good interpersonal relationships, which will help improve career performance.
financial: Being in harmony with Tai Sui (the Grand Duke Jupiter) usually brings more new cooperation opportunities. Furthermore, the auspicious star "San Tai" symbolizes advancement and continuous progress, meaning improved wealth. Even if income remains the same, it will be easier to profit in the Year of the Horse. However, years in harmony with Tai Sui always have uncertainties. Therefore, those born in the Year of the Dog must manage risks carefully. For new investments or projects, they should thoroughly understand the market situation and avoid investing too much capital. Small investments with the aim of generating profits are recommended, as they have a higher chance of success. In addition, the inauspicious star "Guan Fu" also appears in the Year of the Horse. If signing new cooperation agreements, it is essential to seek professional assistance to verify the contract's contents to avoid legal disputes due to carelessness. Business owners should pay special attention to their clients' financial situation, avoiding extending credit or delivery to prevent breach of contract and losses. Furthermore, "Di Jie" symbolizes real estate transactions. Renovations or relocations of residences, offices, or shops may incur additional expenses in the Year of the Horse. It is advisable to set aside funds for unforeseen circumstances. Since there are no obvious lucky stars or benefactors in the Year of the Horse, and "San Tai" is an auspicious star that rises slowly and develops gradually, while "Hua Gai" has the significance of making independent decisions, the investment strategy for the new year should still be mainly stable. Choose reliable medium- to long-term projects, and conduct your own analysis and research before making investment decisions. Do not rely on benefactors or rumors to obtain profits.
health: In the Year of the Horse (2016), health will be relatively stable, but due to the influence of the inauspicious star "Five Ghosts," those born in the Year of the Dog are more prone to suspicion and their overall energy will be relatively low. Therefore, during the Year of the Horse, it is advisable to avoid visiting the sick or attending funerals, and also to avoid going to secluded places such as cemeteries, temples, nightclubs, or bars where there is little sunlight, to avoid unnecessary anxiety. It is recommended that those born in the Year of the Dog attend more birthday banquets or baby showers to attract positive energy, and also enjoy leisure and entertainment at upscale hotels or resorts to help relax. In addition, career development will be relatively smooth in the Year of the Horse, with opportunities for promotion. However, with increased responsibilities and work pressure after promotion, and a lack of cooperation among colleagues, those born in the Year of the Dog may feel a persistent sense of loneliness, which will affect their mood. Therefore, it is recommended to participate in more sports, connect with nature, or choose to take a short trip during the holidays. For those born in the first half of the year, traveling to colder regions is recommended, while those born in the second half should opt for tropical areas. This will not only help them relax but also enhance their fortune through "borrowing local luck." Additionally, due to the influence of the inauspicious star "披头" (Pi Tou), special attention should be paid to family safety and the health of elders. If any discomfort is observed, accompany them to the doctor promptly to prevent minor issues from escalating into major problems. Since the auspicious star "地解" (Di Jie) is present this year, consider taking this opportunity to carry out minor renovations or repairs to the elders' residence, replacing some old appliances, sofas, or bedding. This can also improve the home's and health's fortune.
relationship: The Year of the Horse will bring the auspicious star "San Tai," symbolizing slow progress. For those with partners or married, relationships will generally remain stable. However, the influence of the religiously significant "Hua Gai" star remains. This star represents the imperial parasol used during imperial processions, which, while revered, also conveys an aloof and unapproachable aura, which is not favorable for romantic relationships. Therefore, even with a partner, those born in the Year of the Dog may feel misunderstood, leading to emotional conflict and increased loneliness, requiring more time to nurture the relationship. Furthermore, the arrival of the inauspicious star "Wu Gui" may make those born in the Year of the Dog neurotic, prone to suspicion of their partner, feeling they are being kept in the dark, thus lowering their trust in their spouse. Moreover, disagreements over family matters or child-rearing methods may easily cause dissatisfaction and rifts, necessitating honest communication and calm handling to avoid misunderstandings caused by suspicion that could damage the relationship. Given the current minor conflicts in the relationship, it is recommended that those born in the Year of the Dog cultivate hobbies together with their partners, arrange family gatherings, meals with friends, or trips together to strengthen their bond. As for singles, the situation is similarly difficult to improve. Besides lacking helpful mentors, they tend to enjoy solitude and have a weaker desire for romance, making their chances of finding a suitable partner and ending their single status extremely slim. Since the timing isn't right, it's advisable to focus on career development during the Year of the Horse and not have high expectations for love.
